example.en: I give a gift to you.

definitions.vi: đưa cái gì đó như một món quà
definitions.en: to give something as a gift

notes_md:
**tặng** = to give something as a gift (not just “give,” but give to show care, celebration, or respect).

You use it when the thing is meant as a present: flowers, money, gifts, awards, etc. It sounds warmer and more intentional than **cho** (which just means “give” in general).  


For elders or formal respect, **biếu** can be used instead of *tặng* — it feels more polite and humble.
exampleSentences: Hôm qua anh tặng mẹ một bó hoa nhân ngày sinh nhật.
Mỗi dịp Tết, ông bà thường tặng em bao lì xì đỏ.
Chị dự định sẽ tặng bạn thân một cuốn sách yêu thích.

translation.en: I want to give this gift.

content.sentence: Tôi sẽ ___ cho bạn một món quà.

translation.en: I will give you a gift.

exampleSentencesTranslated: Yesterday, I give as a gift my mother a bouquet of flowers for her birthday.
Every Tet holiday, my grandparents usually give as a gift me red lucky money envelopes.
She plans to give as a gift her best friend a favorite book.
notes:
tặng = to give something as a gift (not just “give,” but give to show care, celebration, or respect).

You use it when the thing is meant as a present: flowers, money, gifts, awards, etc. It sounds warmer and more intentional than cho (which just means “give” in general).  

For elders or formal respect, biếu can be used instead of tặng — it feels more polite and humble.
